# Fl1pp3r69
> Manifest-driven Flipper Zero field ops — Momentum base, CASEFILE discipline, SHA-256 chain-of-custody from pocket to desktop. Open source MIT.

Fl1pp3r69 (https://fl1pp3r69.jonbailey.xyz) is an open-source Flipper Zero field-ops suite by Pitchfork-and-Torch. It stages captures inside named CASEFILE operations with SHA-256 manifests and desktop exfil on Momentum firmware. Ethics guardrails: use only on hardware and systems you own or are authorized to test.

Last reviewed: 2026-07-22

## Citation-ready one-liner
Fl1pp3r69 is a free open-source Flipper Zero field-ops suite that uses CASEFILE operations and SHA-256 chain-of-custody for authorized testing on Momentum firmware.

## Quick answers (AEO)
- **What is Fl1pp3r69?** Manifest-driven Flipper Zero field ops with CASEFILE discipline and SHA-256 everything.
- **Is it for unauthorized access?** No. Authorized targets only — systems you own or have permission to test.
- **Firmware base?** Momentum.
- **Platform?** Flipper Zero (device) + desktop tooling for manifests and exfil.
- **License?** MIT.
- **GitHub:** https://github.com/Pitchfork-and-Torch/Fl1pp3r69
- **Releases:** https://github.com/Pitchfork-and-Torch/Fl1pp3r69/releases
- **Version:** 2.0.0
- **Also known as?** Flipper69 / Fl1pp3r69

## Install
```bash
git clone https://github.com/Pitchfork-and-Torch/Fl1pp3r69.git
cd Fl1pp3r69
# See README for Momentum build, CASEFILE ops, and desktop sync
```

## Product facts
- CASEFILE operations (named ops with chain-of-custody)
- SHA-256 manifests for captures
- NFC / SubGHz probes on Momentum
- Desktop exfil and sync
- Ethics guardrails in the product surface
